spec_match.rationale: The visible explorer now teaches the HTML fragment-navigation surface: id, <a name>, href fragments, location.hash, standards mode, pre-150 quirks mode, and Chrome 150+ quirks mode.

interactive.rationale: DevTools MCP exercised the target value input, fragment input, pre-150 quirks mode button, and Chrome 150+ mode button; the matrix and live demo changed between match and no-match states.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The page now matches the feature index by building fragment links against named/id targets instead of CSS Anchor Positioning anchor-name and position-anchor examples.

edge_cases.rationale: The page shows exact-case matches, case-only mismatches, the old case-insensitive quirks behavior, the new Chrome 150+ behavior, and a live support line for browsers that still have the pre-150 behavior.

references.rationale: The concept page links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5085237991309312 and the feature conformance route links to the WHATWG HTML fragment indicated-element algorithm.

design_system.rationale: The page keeps the existing variable-based design system, removes stale inline onclick handlers, and avoids injecting user-entered values with innerHTML.

summary: Pre-fix DevTools MCP confirmed the page tested <a name> fragment navigation but still displayed CSS Anchor Positioning terms such as anchor-name, position-anchor, and an anchor positioning code block. Post-fix Chrome 148 testing at http://localhost:3000/v150/case-sensitive-anchor-name-matching-in-quirks-mode/anchor-case-explorer/ exercised target/fragment inputs, pre-150 quirks mode, Chrome 150+ mode, exact-case matches, case-only mismatches, and an escaping check with HTML-like input; no DOM injection occurred. Network requests were clean. DevTools reported expected issues from the intentional quirks-mode iframe/hash probe. The conformance route showed 4 PASS, 1 FAIL in Chrome 148, with the failing fragment-navigation-mismatch assertion matching the page's pre-Chrome 150 support warning for this v150 feature.
